proof_sketch: The kernel is launched with a 1D block (dim3 block(BLOCK_SIZE)), so blockDim.y = 1. Original stride = blockDim.x = BLOCK_SIZE (512 for dim_size=16384), mutated stride = blockDim.y = 1. In the final loop, each thread originally processes elements spaced 512 apart; after mutation, each thread processes a contiguous range from its threadIdx.x to dim_size-1. For any output index i, multiple threads compute and write the same value (batch_input[i] - log_sum_exp). Since the subtraction result depends only on the input and the per-block computed log_sum_exp, not on the thread index, all writes yield the same float32 value. Thus the final output is bit-identical regardless of the stride.
